name: Kyoto (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity and the elegance of the old imperial capital
the imperial purple of the old capital

description: A white field charged at the centre with a purple-and-gold emblem depicting the kanji 京 (kyō, 'capital') stylised within a six-petalled flower (gosho-guruma, 'imperial palace wheel').
history: The Kyoto City emblem (gosho-guruma) was officially adopted on 1 October 1960, derived from a design used by the city since 1891. The flag bearing the emblem on a white field has been the city's official flag since the same date. The 'gosho-guruma' design references the courtly carriages of the imperial Heian-period capital and stylises the character 京 ('capital').

features.role: Kyoto City emblem (gosho-guruma) — the kanji 京 within a six-petalled flower
features.symbolism: the gosho-guruma ('imperial palace wheel') design stylises the character 京 ('capital') within a six-petalled court-carriage flower, evoking Kyoto's thousand-year role as Japan's imperial capital (794–1869)
